title: quercus lobata subsp. walteri
intro: Quercus lobata subsp. walteri, also known as California white oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to the western coast of North America. The medicinal properties of Quercus lobata subsp. walteri are attributed to its leaves, which have been used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Quercus lobata subsp. walteri can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various herbal preparations.
